subject: Mathematics
title: The ABC Conjecture
description: Learn The ABC Conjecture in one minute.

hook.text: Three numbers—a, b, and c—hide one of the deepest unsolved puzzles in mathematics.

buildup.text: The ABC conjecture links addition and multiplication in a way nobody expected.

discovery.text: It says when a + b = c, the prime factors of a, b, c are usually large relative to c.

twist.text: In 2012, Shinichi Mochizuki claimed a proof in 500 pages of entirely new mathematics.

climax.text: A decade later, most mathematicians still cannot verify it. The debate continues.

punchline.text: A proof nobody can read might not be a proof at all.

quiz.question: What is unusual about Mochizuki's claimed proof of the ABC Conjecture?
options: Most mathematicians cannot verify the new mathematics it uses
It was only one page long
It was proven by a computer
